title: Introductions and telephone numbers
setting: Two new acquaintances introduce themselves and exchange contact details.

turns.speaker: A
turns.dutch: Hallo, ik ben Teo. Hoe heet jij?
turns.english: Hello, I am Teo. What is your name?

turns.speaker: B
turns.dutch: Ik ben Julia.
turns.english: I am Julia.

turns.speaker: A
turns.dutch: Waar kom je vandaan, Julia?
turns.english: Where are you from, Julia?

turns.speaker: B
turns.dutch: Ik kom uit Argentinië. En jij?
turns.english: I come from Argentina. And you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.dutch: Ik kom uit Rusland. Hoe lang woon je al in Nederland?
turns.english: I come from Russia. How long have you been living in the Netherlands?

turns.speaker: B
turns.dutch: Eén maand. Ik woon in Almere.
turns.english: One month. I live in Almere.

turns.speaker: A
turns.dutch: Heb je een Nederlands telefoonnummer?
turns.english: Do you have a Dutch telephone number?

turns.speaker: B
turns.dutch: Ja, dat heb ik.
turns.english: Yes, I do.

turns.speaker: A
turns.dutch: Wat is je achternaam?
turns.english: What is your last name?

turns.speaker: B
turns.dutch: Fuentes.
turns.english: Fuentes.

turns.speaker: A
turns.dutch: Hoe spel ik dat?
turns.english: How do I spell that?

notes: Normalized from handwritten class dialogue; obvious spelling noise was corrected without changing meaning.

title: A new neighbor
setting: A new resident introduces herself to her neighbors.

turns.speaker: Saida
turns.dutch: Hallo, ik ben Saida. Ik ben de nieuwe buurvrouw.
turns.english: Hello, I am Saida. I am the new female neighbor.

turns.speaker: Saida
turns.dutch: Ik woon sinds twee weken in mijn nieuwe huis.
turns.english: I have lived in my new house for two weeks.

turns.speaker: Saida
turns.dutch: Ik werk in het weekend in een restaurant.
turns.english: I work in a restaurant at the weekend.

turns.speaker: Saida
turns.dutch: Ik ben niet getrouwd en ik heb geen kinderen.
turns.english: I am not married and I have no children.

turns.speaker: Saida
turns.dutch: Ik woon met mijn poes. Ik houd van koken en sporten.
turns.english: I live with my cat. I love cooking and exercising.

turns.speaker: Saida
turns.dutch: Ik wil mijn nieuwe buren graag ontmoeten.
turns.english: I would like to meet my new neighbors.

notes: Normalized from handwritten class dialogue; obvious spelling noise was corrected without changing meaning.

title: At the market
setting: A customer buys groceries from a seller.

turns.speaker: Verkoper
turns.dutch: Goedemiddag, zeg het maar.
turns.english: Good afternoon, what can I get you?

turns.speaker: Klant
turns.dutch: Een kilo aardappels en zes eieren, alstublieft.
turns.english: A kilo of potatoes and six eggs, please.

turns.speaker: Verkoper
turns.dutch: Anders nog iets?
turns.english: Anything else?

turns.speaker: Klant
turns.dutch: Nee, dat is alles. Kan ik pinnen?
turns.english: No, that is everything. Can I pay by card?

turns.speaker: Verkoper
turns.dutch: Natuurlijk. Fijne dag!
turns.english: Of course. Have a nice day!

notes: Normalized from handwritten class dialogue; obvious spelling noise was corrected without changing meaning.

title: Receiving a visitor
setting: Someone welcomes a visitor into their home.

turns.speaker: Host
turns.dutch: Kom binnen. Geef je jas maar hier.
turns.english: Come in. Just give me your coat.

turns.speaker: Visitor
turns.dutch: Dank je wel.
turns.english: Thank you.

turns.speaker: Host
turns.dutch: Wil je koffie of thee?
turns.english: Would you like coffee or tea?

turns.speaker: Visitor
turns.dutch: Het maakt me niet uit. Doe maar thee.
turns.english: I don't mind. Tea is fine.

turns.speaker: Host
turns.dutch: Met melk en suiker?
turns.english: With milk and sugar?

turns.speaker: Visitor
turns.dutch: Melk graag, maar doe maar geen suiker.
turns.english: Milk please, but no sugar.

notes: Normalized from handwritten class dialogue; obvious spelling noise was corrected without changing meaning.
